title: astragalus tener var. tener
intro: Astragalus tener var. tener, also known as Mexican milk vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and enhancing the body's natural defenses against illness and infection. Astragalus tener var. tener is typically prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being.
